DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action 2, 3, 4, 5, 7 and 8 as to defendants WAYMO AUTO LLC, WAYMO HOLDING, INC., and WAYMO LLC (collectively, "Waymo"). Plaintiff has leave to plead facts (e.g. Waymo controlled the means and manner of plaintiff's work) that show a employment or joint employment relationship. (Vernon v. State of Cal. (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 114, 126-127 [sustaining demurrer where there were insufficient allegations regarding a joint employment relationship].) It is not enough to note, as plaintiff does, that joint employment may be a viable theory (it is not pled in the complaint). Facts, such as that the defendant "carries out FEHA-regulated activities on behalf of an employer" should also be alleged. Raines v. U.S. Healthworks Medical Group (2023) 15 Cal.5th 268, 312 Cal.Rptr.3d 301, 318. Plaintiff cites Burke v. Zanes (1961) 193 Cal.App.2d 773, 780 suggesting this permits general allegations of an "employer-employee relation". This is a gross mis-citation of the venerable case, which held that absent any pleading of an employment relation, a defense based on gross negligence was unavailable. Plaintiff also has leave to plead ultimate facts showing how Waymo knew or should have known that Mr. Gehlot was unfit to perform his work. Plaintiff is reminded that allegations on information and belief do not permit pure speculation. A "plaintiff may allege on information and belief any matters that are not within his personal knowledge, if he has information leading him to believe that the allegations are true. and thus a pleading made on information and belief is insufficient if it merely asserts the facts so alleged without alleging such information that leads the plaintiff to believe that the allegations are true." Gomes v. Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1149, 1158-1159 (cleaned up). The demurrer is overruled on cause of action 8 as to defendant SECURITAS SECURITY SERVICES USA, INC. Plaintiff pleads sufficient ultimate facts that the work environment was intolerable and led to her constructive discharge. "Whether conditions were so intolerable as to justify a reasonable employee's decision to resign is normally a question of fact." (Scotch v. Art Inst. of Cal. (2009) 173 Cal. App. 4th 986, 1022.) Any new complaint must be filed and served not later than November 10, 2023.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CEK)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

payments.description: REQ OR STIP AND ORDER W/O HEARING
